# Extracao: main list 2

- **Fonte:** main list 2.txt
- **Tipo:** TXT
- **Tamanho:** 0 MB

---

Subject: Are they really secrets?

A couple of days ago I told you about a
report that I had just released on "19
Secrets" about writing copy that sells.

In the next 24 hours that report was
downloaded THOUSANDS of times.

And it generated all kinds of positive
reviews and comments. I got lots of emails
thanking me for the report. And a bunch more
people posted really nice comments on our
blog.

In fact, you can see the comments here:

http://xxxxxxxx


However... to be honest, there were a few
naysayers. A couple of people (actually, it
was three people) sent in grumpy emails,
telling me that there weren't any "secrets"
in the report. And they said they already
knew everything in the report.

And maybe they have a point... maybe I should
have called the report "19 Fundamental Truths
About Copywriting That You Should Never Forget."

Because that's what they are... fundamental
truths.

This is the deal... I have a revolutionary
new online copywriting course coming out very
soon. It contains everything I know about
copy (and I know a lot - I have written 19
different sales letters that generated at
least $1 million each.)

But before I finished that course, I decided
to survey my readers about copywriting to
make sure I left no stone unturned.

And when I saw the results of that survey...
well, that's when I decided to put together
my "19 Secrets" special report - people
were crying for help, and I knew I could
clear a few things up.

So I literally stayed up all night on Tuesday
writing this report... and frankly, I am
pretty happy with it. 

In particular, Secret #5 and Secret #18 (they
are closely related, really two sides of a
coin) were HUGE for me when I learned them
from Dan Kennedy. I can vividly remember
EXACTLY where I was when I first learned
those two "secrets".

So are they REALLY secrets? They certainly
were for me at that time. But I tell you
what... even if you ALREADY know those two
"secrets" (or the other 17 in the report),
these are principles that we ALL need to
remember. 

So have it your way... consider them
"secrets" or consider them "fundamental
truths". Either way, they should be among the
cornerstones for all your copywriting. 

Did I leave some secrets out of the report?
Of course I did. It's only 18 pages long, and
I can only fit so much in there. And I am not
in the business of giving away the ENTIRE
store.

That's it for now... I am going to leave that
report available at least a few more days. If
you haven't gotten your copy yet, then you
can get it here:

http://www. link to squeeze page


best regards,
Bill Glazer

P.S. One mistake I see seasoned marketers
make OFTEN is they get "too smart for the
room"... in other words, they achieve a
certain level of knowledge, and they think
they have heard it all. They stop studying,
they stop learning. They lose patience when
ever they think they have heard something
before. This is a dangerous mind set to have,
and you want to avoid it.

http://www. link to squeeze page
